tasks.title: [R11-5.B.1] Create .claude/skills/blueprint/** from templates/skills/blueprint/**

Phase: Revision 11 — Skill-Based Agent Surface
Gate/Stream: Stream B — Local Skill Installation
Duration: 0.75
Dependencies: Gate

Detail:
Create .claude/skills/blueprint/** by copying the authoritative local payload from templates/skills/blueprint/**. The installed tree must include SKILL.md, all 20 reference/*.md module mirrors, reference/anti-patterns.md, and scripts/load-context.mjs.
tasks.author: Codex
tasks.implementation_notes: Made the Blueprint skill payload a tracked dogfood exception under .claude/skills/blueprint/** by updating .gitignore to ignore other .claude state while explicitly re-including the Blueprint skill subtree. The checked-in payload remains byte-identical to templates/skills/blueprint and keeps the local install reproducible after a clean checkout or merge/cleanup.

tasks.implementation_notes: Changed the Stream B loader verification to run from portable temp fixtures instead of the repo root. The test now seeds the tracked .claude/skills/blueprint payload into each fixture, checks the no-tracker brief (`not initialised — run blueprint init`) for clean tracked-checkout parity, and separately checks the initialized brief (`initialised at docs/.blueprint/tasks.db`) when the fixture includes a tracker DB file.

tasks.title: [R11-5.C.2] Execute one small tweak through installed skill workflow

Duration: 1.5

The tweak must be real but low-risk; preferred target is cleanup of stale docs/conventions.md worked-example references in docs/core/tweak-planning.md and mirrored skill/template surfaces if those references still exist. Follow the change-first tweak loop, verify the change, and create the next docs/tweaks/tweak-<n>-<slug>.md post-hoc record.

Added C.2 doc-contract coverage in tests/revision-11/phase-5/dogfood-tweak-record.test.ts.

Verification passed: rg stale worked-example tokens across touched tweak surfaces returned no matches; diff -q passed for docs core/template mirror and skill template/repo-root/local install mirrors; npm run test -- tests/revision-11/phase-5/dogfood-tweak-record.test.ts tests/revision-11/phase-5/helpers.test.ts tests/revision-11/stream-a/reference-mirrors.test.ts tests/revision-8/stream-a/tweak-planning-rewrite.test.ts tests/revision-8/stream-c/tweak-planning-mas207-contract.test.ts tests/revision-10/stream-b/tweak-planning-antipatterns-shape.test.ts passed 106 tests.

Full npm test was run and failed only on failures outside C.2 scope: stale legacy root-routing assertions from prior revision tests after Phase 5 root entry-point skill-mode conversion, plus board E2E startup timeouts. No failure was attributable to the C.2 touched files or tests.

Commit: 5e3001f R11-5.C.2: dogfood tweak complete, ready for review.

tasks.title: [BUG] Trim skill-mode AGENTS scaffold
tasks.description:
Symptom: blueprint init skill mode scaffolds AGENTS.md with blueprint-cli-specific ProjectConventions, including this repository technology stack and release process.
Expected: skill-mode AGENTS.md should contain only the minimal Blueprint skill invocation guidance, roughly the first 4-5 lines.
Root Cause: templates/skill/AGENTS.md and sibling skill-mode entry-point templates include the repo-specific ProjectConventions snippet, and tests currently require it.
Fix Approach: Add regression coverage that skill-mode entry-point templates exclude ProjectConventions, then trim the templates to the minimal invocation text.
Files Affected: templates/skill/AGENTS.md, templates/skill/CLAUDE.md, templates/skill/GEMINI.md, templates/skill/QWEN.md, tests/revision-11/stream-c/skill-mode-entry-point-stubs.test.ts, tests/revision-11/stream-d/scaffold-engine.test.ts

Reproduction:
Inspect templates/skill/AGENTS.md or run skill-mode scaffold generation and observe blueprint-cli-specific ProjectConventions in the generated AGENTS.md.
tasks.author: Codex
tasks.implementation_notes: Fixed skill-mode init scaffold leakage by trimming templates/skill/{CLAUDE,AGENTS,GEMINI,QWEN}.md to the minimal Blueprint skill invocation stub. Added regression coverage in tests/revision-11/stream-c/skill-mode-entry-point-stubs.test.ts and tests/revision-11/stream-d/scaffold-engine.test.ts so templates and generated stubs must not contain ProjectConventions or blueprint-cli-specific stack/release details. Updated older R11 contracts in tests/revision-11/stream-a/conventions-sunset.test.ts, tests/revision-11/stream-c/alignment-doc-contract.test.ts, tests/revision-11/phase-5/helpers.ts, and tests/revision-11/phase-5/root-entry-points.test.ts to separate project-root dogfood conventions from generic init templates. Verification: focused affected R11 tests passed; npm run typecheck passed; clean full npm test passed with 1321 passed and 2 skipped.

comments.body: Baseline inventory: root CLAUDE/AGENTS/GEMINI/QWEN are legacy-mode with <SessionStart>, <ModuleRouting>, <HardRules>, and docs/conventions.md load guidance. templates/skill has four skill-mode entry points plus _project-conventions snippet. templates/skills/blueprint and skills/blueprint each have 23 files. .claude has 0 files; .claude/skills/blueprint is absent/ready for Stream B. No file modifications made for inventory. Board task API reachable on 127.0.0.1:7300; /project currently returns project_not_found after snapshot recovery, so execution used supported task endpoints only.
comments.author: Codex

comments.body: Added tests/revision-11/phase-5/helpers.ts and helpers.test.ts. Helpers enumerate root entry point to templates/skill pairings, derive .claude/skills/blueprint install paths from shared SKILL_PAYLOAD_INVENTORY, and enumerate active cross-reference surfaces while excluding archival docs/milestones history. TDD evidence: helpers.test.ts first failed because ./helpers was missing, then passed after implementation. Verification: npm run test -- tests/revision-11/phase-5/helpers.test.ts; npm run test -- tests/revision-11/phase-5/helpers.test.ts tests/revision-11/gate-4.0/skill-payload-inventory.test.ts tests/revision-11/stream-a/skill-payload-mirror-contract.test.ts; npm run typecheck. No dedicated lint script exists in package.json, so typecheck plus focused Vitest coverage were used for touched files.
comments.author: Codex

comments.body: Clean review: baseline inventory evidence is recorded in tracker comments and satisfies the Gate R11-5.0 pre-change-state requirement. Targeted review found no blocking issues.
comments.author: reviewer

comments.body: Clean review: Phase 5 helper implementation matches the Gate R11-5.0.2 test plan, reuses the shared skill payload inventory, and the targeted helper tests pass.
comments.author: reviewer

comments.body:
The installed .claude payload is only present as ignored local state, not as tracked branch content. `.gitignore` ignores `.claude/` (line 32), `git ls-tree -r HEAD .claude/skills/blueprint` is empty, and `git diff main...HEAD` only adds the helper/test files. That means merge + worktree cleanup would leave main without `.claude/skills/blueprint/**`, so the task does not satisfy the Stream B acceptance criteria in a durable way or unblock downstream worktrees.
Why: the local dogfood install must be reproducible from the reviewed branch state, not only from ignored filesystem state.
comments.author: reviewer

comments.body: Task moved to REWORK. After corrections, the agent must follow the canonical REWORK -> IN-PROGRESS -> IN-REVIEW transition per docs/core/tracker.md before this task can reach DONE.

comments.body: Reworked the task through the canonical review loop and kept the payload reproducible in branch state; the skill subtree is now the tracked dogfood exception under .claude/skills/blueprint/**, while the rest of .claude remains ignored.

comments.author: reviewer

comments.body:
The new coverage hard-codes `.claude/skills/blueprint` as a repo fixture (`LOCAL_ROOT`) and even seeds the negative cases by copying from that local tree. Because `.claude/` is ignored, the tests only pass in a worktree where someone has manually installed the skill. In a tracked-only export of commit `960f8f7`, `vitest run tests/revision-11/stream-b/local-skill-install.test.ts` fails 4/4 with ENOENT / MODULE_NOT_FOUND because `.claude/skills/blueprint/**` is absent.
Why: regression coverage has to run from tracked repository state so CI, fresh checkouts, and downstream worktrees see the same result as the author machine.

comments.body:
tests/revision-11/phase-5/dogfood-tweak-record.test.ts:16 hard-codes the entire docs/tweaks directory with toEqual([...]). This proves tweak 6 exists today, but it also forbids any future valid tweak-7 record unless this C.2 test is edited.
Why: Future normal Blueprint tweak work would break an unrelated C.2 regression test, making the suite brittle and blocking legitimate additions to docs/tweaks. Please assert that tweak-6 exists at the correct next number relative to the prior state, or that no gaps exist through 6, without requiring there to be no later tweak files.

comments.body: Task moved to REWORK. After corrections, the agent must follow the canonical REWORK → IN-PROGRESS → IN-REVIEW transition per docs/core/tracker.md before this task can reach DONE.

The active cross-reference audit helper omits the release docs (`docs/release-contract.md` and `docs/releasing.md`) from `ACTIVE_CROSS_REFERENCE_STATIC_FILES`, even though Stream D calls release docs part of the active install-guidance surface.
Why: stale or conflicting skill-install guidance can be reintroduced in release-facing docs without failing the audit, so the D.1 repo-wide active-surface audit boundary is incomplete.

Reviewer clarification: the blocking issue is in `tests/revision-11/phase-5/helpers.ts:10`: `ACTIVE_CROSS_REFERENCE_STATIC_FILES` does not include `docs/release-contract.md` or `docs/releasing.md`.
Why: Stream D names release docs as an active surface for install guidance, so the audit must include those files before D.1 can be considered complete.
comments.author: reviewer

comments.body:
The Stream D regression coverage does not assert that `docs/release-contract.md` and `docs/releasing.md` are included in `getActiveCrossReferenceFiles()`, and the negative fixtures only inject stale guidance into `CLAUDE.md`.
Why: D.3 is supposed to protect active install docs from conflicting Phase 4 skill-install guidance; without release-doc coverage, those files can drift while the audit suite remains green.

comments.body:
Rereview found a Stream D-attributable full-suite failure: the cross-reference cleanup changes the canonical <ProjectConventions> snippet (for example templates/skill/_project-conventions.snippet.md now says root docs are docs/project-progress.md, docs/prd.md, and docs/srs.md, and points Agent Tools at .claude/skills/blueprint/reference/), but tests/revision-11/stream-a/conventions-sunset.test.ts still expects the old R11 Phase 3 snapshot. `npm test` fails at T-R11-3.A.4.2.
Why: the Phase 5 D.1 test plan explicitly says any <ProjectConventions> content change must forward-update existing R11 Phase 3 snapshot expectations. The active audit is not complete while a D-introduced active-surface change leaves the existing contract suite red.
comments.author: reviewer

comments.body:
The new Stream D coverage still lets the targeted Phase 5 suite pass while the existing ProjectConventions snapshot contract fails under `npm test` (T-R11-3.A.4.2 in tests/revision-11/stream-a/conventions-sunset.test.ts).
Why: D.3 is the regression boundary for active cross-reference changes; it should either update the existing snapshot expectation when the canonical snippet intentionally changes, or add coverage that catches this companion-update requirement before the full suite does.
